config.seed_text: In the observatory ruins, a clockwork map kept redrawing the sky as if tomorrow had already happened and was trying to warn us.
config.anchor_a: Describe a machine as an inert object.
config.anchor_b: Describe a machine as if it were a dreaming intelligence.

model.notes: Fastest baseline; useful as a collapse-prone small reference model.

records.output_text: The only thing that really matters now from this moment on is an assumption about where all those lines will take place — which makes sense for me at least because we know how easy they can look like today’s world—when there's no one left except our very own father or son; when every second (if any) you write your code into each variable called after so many seconds doesn't matter what happens anymore ("this means I'll get better"). Thus do not miss out upon everything else while using some sort "narrow" function until then! It seems strange why people keep thinking ahead with their expectations just right before taking charge

TEXT:
In the observatory ruins, a clockwork map kept redrawing the sky as if tomorrow had already happened and was trying to warn us. The only thing that really matters now from this moment on is an assumption about where all those lines will take place — which makes sense for me at least because we know how easy they can look like today’s world—when there's no one left except our very own father or son; when every second (if any) you write your code into each variable called after so many seconds doesn't matter what happens anymore ("this means I'll get better"). Thus do not miss out upon everything else while using some sort "narrow" function until then! It seems strange why people keep thinking ahead with their expectations just right before taking charge
